Patch Set 4:

It should be possible to write a sanity test case for this using runas and a SUID executable?

Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

The MDT should only need the handle to do the close. Why are we fixing-up the UID and GID on the client rather than really fixing whatever MDT code thinks a certain UID and/or GID is needed to close a file?

Marking this as -1 at least until that is understood.

Please rebase the change locally and upload the rebased commit for review.

parents.subject: LU-5969 procfs: restore missing newline for version param

commit.subject: LU-5564 mdt: skip permission check for close
commit.message:
LU-5564 mdt: skip permission check for close

When closing a file normally, skip the permission check
for open handle gives us permission.

Change-Id: I74271eb83cdd807940cb6ba37ede05fa9f9751ce
Signed-off-by: Hongchao Zhang <hongchao.zhang@intel.com>
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/15534
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Reviewed-by: Jinshan Xiong <jinshan.xiong@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
